reasoning_model_1: The model generates the text 'Oh my god, I won the lottery! It is incredible!' (expanding 'It's' to 'It is'). reasoning_model_2: The model generates the text 'Oh my god, I won the lottery! It is incredible!' (expanding 'It's' to 'It is'). The model successfully adheres to the 'very high pitch' constraint. model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. The user specifically requested the sentence to be read with a 'very high pitch'. Model 1 ignored this constraint, delivering the line in a standard, neutral-to-medium pitch that sounded casual and unexcited. Model 2, in contrast, effectively applied the requested feature, generating audio with a distinctly high frequency and an excited tone that matched the context of the script. Model 2 followed the prompt's specific paralinguistic instruction, whereas Model 1 did not.
